title: Inside Zcash's new node that targets Visa-scale privacy at 50,000 transactions per second
content: The new Zakura client is the first live piece of a plan to take Zcash from roughly one private transaction per second to payment-network scale.
summary: Zcash has launched its new Zakura client, the first step in a plan to scale its private transaction capabilities from approximately one per second to 50,000 transactions per second. This initiative aims to achieve Visa-level privacy at payment-network scale.

title: Tokenization has become a strategic priority for 84% of financial firms
content: A Broadridge survey found Wall Street is accelerating tokenization efforts while betting on hybrid markets where digital and traditional assets coexist.
summary: A Broadridge survey indicates that 84% of financial firms have made tokenization a strategic priority. Wall Street is actively accelerating its tokenization efforts, anticipating the rise of hybrid markets where digital and traditional assets coexist.

title: ESMA adds 14 new CASPs to MiCA register as licensing slows
content: ESMA adds 14 CASPs to its MiCA register, with banks and Ripple Payments Europe among the new entries as total providers reach 294.
summary: The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) has added 14 new Crypto-Asset Service Providers (CASPs) to its MiCA register, bringing the total count to 294. Among the new entries are banks and Ripple Payments Europe, indicating ongoing regulatory compliance within the crypto sector.

title: Bitcoin's biggest advocate, Michael Saylor, says new plan to clean up the blockchain is 'a bad idea'
content: Michael Saylor argues that a new proposal, BIP-110, to temporarily block "spam" data from the Bitcoin blockchain would undermine the network's neutrality and create a dangerous precedent for censorship.
summary: Michael Saylor, a prominent Bitcoin advocate, has criticized a new proposal, BIP-110, which aims to temporarily block "spam" data from the Bitcoin blockchain. He argues it would undermine the network's neutrality and create a dangerous precedent for censorship.

title: AI is destroying the internet. Math is our only hope.
content: The rise of autonomous AI agents necessitates the utilization of zero-knowledge proofs, argues Brian Trunzo, chief growth officer at Succinct Labs.
summary: The article highlights the urgent need for zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs) to address the challenges posed by the proliferation of autonomous AI agents, arguing that AI is negatively impacting the internet. Brian Trunzo of Succinct Labs suggests that advanced mathematical solutions like ZKPs are essential for AI's future integrity.

title: Tether's USDT hits 2-year countdown threatening its position on U.S. crypto platforms
content: The GENIUS Act has hit its first anniversary without U.S. regulators yet meeting deadlines to write regulations, though rules will be in full effect by July 2028.
summary: The GENIUS Act has reached its first anniversary, with U.S. regulators yet to meet deadlines for writing crucial regulations. Despite the current delays, the full rules are set to be in effect by July 2028, potentially threatening the position of stablecoins like Tether's USDT on U.S. crypto platforms.

title: Bitcoin’s quantum problem gets a recovery tool, but not for Satoshi’s 1.1 million coins
content: Project Eleven says it has funded a proof that lets a wallet's own key-derivation path stand in as ownership after quantum computers can forge its signatures. It runs in 243 milliseconds on a laptop.
summary: Project Eleven has developed a recovery tool for Bitcoin that allows a wallet's key-derivation path to serve as proof of ownership, specifically designed to counter signature forgery by future quantum computers. This solution runs quickly on a laptop but does not apply to Satoshi Nakamoto's 1.1 million Bitcoin.

title: France orders country's internet service providers to block Polymarket
content: The regulator cited concerns over addictive mechanics, a lack of self-exclusion tools, and a high volume of French users bypassing previous financial restrictions.
summary: France's regulator has ordered internet service providers to block access to Polymarket, a decentralized prediction market. The decision stems from concerns regarding addictive mechanics, the absence of self-exclusion tools, and a large number of French users circumventing existing financial restrictions.

title: DOG Mode explains Bitcoin's next governance fight
content: Leonidas' DOG Mode client challenges Bitcoin's default relay policies, reopening a philosophical debate over censorship, free markets and who really governs the network.
summary: Leonidas's DOG Mode client is challenging Bitcoin's default relay policies, sparking a renewed governance fight. This move reopens a philosophical debate about censorship, free markets, and the fundamental control over the Bitcoin network.

title: Stripe and Swift race to control the next generation of global payments infrastructure
content: Crypto and blockchain experts say this week's moves show the two established finance companies are increasingly competing for control of the infrastructure behind digital payments.
summary: Stripe and Swift are increasingly competing to control the infrastructure behind digital payments, a race highlighted by their recent moves. Crypto and blockchain experts note this competition for the next generation of global payments infrastructure.

title: Inside Robinhood’s high-stakes bet to onboard millions of casual users onto decentralized finance
content: The company says its advantage lies in bringing retail customers onchain, but early activity remains dominated by memecoins, while its original tokenization vision remains small.
summary: Robinhood is undertaking a high-stakes initiative to onboard 10 million casual users into decentralized finance (DeFi). While the company aims to leverage its retail customer base for widespread onchain adoption, early activity is primarily driven by memecoins, and its larger tokenization vision has seen limited progress.

title: Japan's SBI Group is building Asia's first cross-border digital asset empire
content: The securities giant announced today the consolidation of Singapore-based Coinhako as part of a rapid regional expansion that includes a tokenization partnership with Ondo Finance.
summary: Japan's SBI Group is rapidly expanding its digital asset operations across Asia, aiming to establish the region's first cross-border digital asset empire. This expansion includes the consolidation of Singapore-based Coinhako and a new tokenization partnership with Ondo Finance.

title: Bitcoin faces fresh headwinds as China’s Kimi beats Claude, GPT in coding benchmark
content: Moonshot's Kimi K3 took the top spot in frontend coding away from Claude and OpenAI, and it's free. Semiconductor stocks fell, and crypto fell with them.
summary: Moonshot's Kimi K3, a free AI model, has surpassed Claude and OpenAI's GPT in a frontend coding benchmark. This development, alongside a decline in semiconductor stocks, coincides with a fall in crypto prices, presenting new challenges for Bitcoin.

title: Saylor turns up heat with ‘110 reasons’ why BIP-110 is a bad idea
content: The man in control of the biggest Bitcoin corporate treasury said he shares the objectives but disagrees about the remedy detailed in the proposed temporary fork.
summary: Michael Saylor, who oversees the largest corporate Bitcoin treasury, has voiced strong opposition to the proposed temporary fork BIP-110. He stated that while he shares the objectives, he disagrees with the remedy detailed in the proposal.

title: US agencies miss GENIUS Act deadline for final stablecoin rules
content: US regulators failed to finalize implementing regulations by the GENIUS Act’s one-year deadline, issuing 10 proposed rules instead.
summary: US regulators missed the one-year deadline set by the GENIUS Act to finalize implementing regulations for stablecoins. Instead of issuing final rules, they released 10 proposed rules, indicating ongoing regulatory development.

title: French gambling regulator orders ISPs to block Polymarket
content: Autorité nationale des jeux has ordered France’s internet service providers to geoblock Polymarket, citing illegal gambling and market manipulation concerns.
summary: France's gambling authority has ordered the country's internet service providers to geoblock Polymarket. This regulatory action is in response to concerns over illegal gambling and potential market manipulation on the platform.

title: FTX to distribute $900M to creditors in fifth payment round
content: The FTX Recovery Trust and company have distributed about $10 billion since the exchange filed for bankruptcy in November 2022, leaving users cut off from their funds.
summary: FTX is distributing $900M to creditors in its fifth payment round, as part of its efforts to reimburse users who were cut off from their funds after the exchange filed for bankruptcy in 2022. The FTX Recovery Trust has distributed about $10 billion so far. The latest payout is a significant step towards compensating affected users.

title: Consensys unknowingly outsourced developer work to North Korean
content: Through an introduction with a “reputable third-party service provider,“ the company took on a developer who, as part of an investigation, was revealed to be tied to North Korea.
summary: Consensys, a company, unknowingly hired a developer tied to North Korea through a third-party service provider. The discovery was made during an investigation, highlighting potential security risks. This incident raises concerns about the vetting process of external contractors.

title: Crypto Biz: When dollars disappear, stablecoins step in
content: Bolivia moves to recognize USDT amid a dollar shortage, while Bitcoin miners’ AI ambitions face fresh investor scrutiny.
summary: Bolivia is moving to recognize USDT due to a dollar shortage, and Bitcoin miners are facing scrutiny over their AI ambitions. This development highlights the growing role of stablecoins in addressing financial shortages. The intersection of cryptocurrency and AI is also under examination as investors reevaluate miners' AI plans.

title: SBI acquires Singaporean crypto platform Coinhako after MAS approval
content: SBI Holdings received regulatory approval to acquire a majority stake in Singapore-based crypto exchange Coinhako as it expands into stablecoins, onchain finance and tokenized assets.
summary: SBI Holdings has acquired a majority stake in Singapore-based crypto exchange Coinhako after receiving regulatory approval from the MAS, expanding into stablecoins and onchain finance. This move marks SBI's expansion into new areas of cryptocurrency. The acquisition is expected to help SBI grow its presence in the crypto market.

title: Dutch court declares crypto platform Knaken bankrupt over missing funds
content: The Rotterdam court said bankruptcy was necessary to ensure an orderly settlement because the company lacked enough assets to fully repay users.
summary: A Dutch court has declared crypto platform Knaken bankrupt due to missing funds, requiring an orderly settlement for users. The company lacks sufficient assets to fully repay users. The bankruptcy aims to ensure a structured repayment process.

title: Stablecoin growth will erode bank deposits, says ECB’s Cipollone
content: ECB’s Piero Cipollone said stablecoin adoption could erode bank deposits, but the digital euro will keep banks at the center of payments.
summary: The ECB's Piero Cipollone stated that widespread adoption of stablecoins could lead to a decline in bank deposits, but introduced the digital euro as a potential solution to maintain banks' central role in payments. This development highlights the growing impact of cryptocurrencies on traditional banking systems. The ECB is considering the digital euro to stay relevant.

title: UK sentences 2 hackers tied to $115M crypto ransom scheme
content: The pair pleaded guilty after investigators linked them to the Scattered Spider cybercrime group, which US prosecutors say extorted dozens of companies.
summary: Two hackers in the UK have pleaded guilty to being part of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group, which US prosecutors say extorted dozens of companies in a $115M crypto ransom scheme. The hackers were linked to the group through investigations. The scheme affected multiple companies, with the exact number of victims not specified.

title: MacOS malware hijacks Telegram sessions, targets crypto wallets: SlowMist
content: A macOS malware steals credentials to hijack Telegram sessions, decrypt cryptocurrency wallets or trick users into entering their wallet recovery phrases through fake applications.
summary: A new macOS malware has been discovered that steals credentials to hijack Telegram sessions and target crypto wallets, allowing hackers to decrypt wallets or trick users into revealing recovery phrases. The malware uses fake applications to deceive users. This malware poses a significant threat to cryptocurrency users who use Telegram and macOS devices.

title: Bitcoin ETFs add $368M in three-day buying streak
content: US spot Bitcoin ETFs attracted $79.2 million on Thursday, lifting their three-day inflow total to about $368 million as Bitcoin attempted a price recovery.
summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs have experienced a three-day buying streak, accumulating approximately $368 million in inflows. This includes $79.2 million attracted on Thursday, coinciding with Bitcoin's attempt at price recovery.

title: Bitcoin liquidity clusters determine BTC’s price direction as futures flow fuels price
content: Bitcoin futures traders are propping up the current market move, while liquidation heatmap data provides hints on where BTC price may go next.
summary: Bitcoin futures traders are influencing the current market trend, and liquidation heatmap data may indicate the future price direction of BTC. This data provides insight into potential price movements. The relationship between futures flow and price is a key factor in determining BTC's price direction.

title: Citadel Securities invests $400M in Crypto.com at $20B valuation
content: Crypto exchanges have increasingly positioned themselves as bridges between digital asset markets and traditional finance.
summary: Citadel Securities has invested $400M in Crypto.com, valuing the company at $20B. This investment reflects the growing connection between digital asset markets and traditional finance. Crypto exchanges are increasingly acting as bridges between these two markets.

title: France orders internet providers to block Polymarket ahead of World Cup final
content: The order cuts French users off ahead of the World Cup final, which will settle the largest event markets in the industry's history.
summary: France has instructed internet service providers to block access to Polymarket for its users, effective before the World Cup final. This action will prevent French users from participating in or observing the settlement of the platform's largest historical event markets, which are tied to the final match.

title: US regulators miss GENIUS Act’s one-year deadline for final stablecoin rules
content: The delay does not postpone the law’s Jan. 18, 2027 effective date, leaving regulators and stablecoin issuers with a compressed implementation window.
summary: US regulators have missed the one-year deadline for finalizing stablecoin rules under the GENIUS Act. Despite the delay in issuing rules, the law's effective date of January 18, 2027, remains unchanged, leaving a compressed window for implementation by regulators and stablecoin issuers.

title: UNI burn poised to grow as Uniswap governance votes on v4 fees and Robinhood Chain expansion
content: Both proposals route new fees into the UNI burn system created under December's "UNIfication" overhaul, with voting open from July 19 through July 26.
summary: Uniswap's governance is voting on proposals to implement v4 fees and expand to Robinhood Chain, with both proposals routing new fees into the UNI burn system. The voting period is from July 19 to July 26. This move aims to grow the UNI burn, which was created under the UNIfication overhaul in December.

title: Bank of America taps new leaders to bridge crypto, AI and traditional finance
content: Sonali Theisen was named head of BofA's global digital assets platform, while Kevin Milsom was tapped to serve as head of AI transformation
summary: Bank of America has appointed new leaders to oversee its digital assets platform and AI transformation, aiming to bridge the gap between crypto, AI, and traditional finance. Sonali Theisen will lead the global digital assets platform, while Kevin Milsom will head AI transformation. This move highlights the bank's efforts to integrate emerging technologies into its operations.

title: FTX to distribute roughly $900 million to creditors in fifth wave of payouts
content: So far the company's bankruptcy estate has distributed nearly $10 billion to creditors and other claimants since repayments began in 2025.
summary: FTX is distributing around $900 million to creditors in its fifth wave of payouts, with nearly $10 billion already distributed since repayments started in 2025. This marks a significant step in the company's bankruptcy proceedings. The payouts are part of FTX's efforts to settle its debts.

title: Trezor exec pushes back on ZachXBT claim that hardware wallets are ‘complete garbage’
content: "I do not advise using them for important tasks like signing transactions or storing funds," ZachXBT wrote this week.
summary: A debate has emerged between ZachXBT and a Trezor executive regarding the security and reliability of hardware wallets, with ZachXBT claiming they are 'complete garbage' and advising against their use for important tasks. The Trezor executive pushed back on this claim. The discussion highlights concerns about the security of cryptocurrency storage.

title: Bitcoin ETFs could mirror gold’s history of ‘spectacular gains’ and ‘painful drawdowns,’ analyst says
content: Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas compared IBIT’s short time above $100 billion with GLD’s precipitous rise in 2011.
summary: An analyst compared the rise of Bitcoin ETFs to the history of gold ETFs, noting that like gold, Bitcoin could see spectacular gains and painful drawdowns. The comparison was made between IBIT and GLD, with the former briefly surpassing $100 billion. This analysis suggests a potentially volatile future for Bitcoin ETFs.

title: Crypto-style derivatives reach AI compute ahead of planned CME and ICE futures: Bernstein
content: Crypto-style perps and prediction markets hit AI compute ahead of CME and ICE, with regulated GPU futures targeted for late 2026.
summary: Crypto-style derivatives have reached AI compute, with regulated GPU futures expected to launch in late 2026, ahead of planned futures from CME and ICE. This development combines elements of cryptocurrency and artificial intelligence. The launch is targeted for late 2026.

title: Taiwan sentences BitShine crypto exchange ringleader to 22 years in $39 million fraud case: reports
content: The defendant reportedly led a criminal group that operated under the guise of BitShine, which was once registered with the FSC.
summary: The ringleader of BitShine crypto exchange has been sentenced to 22 years in prison for a $39 million fraud case in Taiwan. The group operated under the guise of BitShine, which was once registered with the FSC. The sentence is a significant outcome for a crypto-related fraud case.

title: UK gang jailed over $5.4 million crypto scam posing as police
content: The gang impersonated police officers and used convincing fake police websites to steal more than £4M in cryptocurrency from eight victims.
summary: A UK gang has been jailed for posing as police officers and stealing over $5.4 million in cryptocurrency from eight victims using fake police websites. The gang impersonated police to gain the trust of their victims. The scam resulted in significant financial losses for the victims.

title: USDT vs USDC: Comparing the Two Largest Stablecoins
content: USDT and USDC are stablecoins pegged one-to-one to the U.S. dollar, each backed by reserves covering every token in circulation. Tether’s USDT is the largest stablecoin, with the deepest liquidity across global exchanges. Circle’s USDC is the second-largest, with Circle staking its reputation on being a publicly traded company, with frequent audits, U.S. and E.U. […]
summary: The article compares USDT and USDC, the two largest stablecoins pegged to the US dollar, discussing their differences in liquidity and audit transparency. USDT has the deepest liquidity, while USDC is backed by a publicly traded company with frequent audits. This comparison is significant for understanding the stablecoin market.

title: SEC vs CFTC: Who Regulates Crypto?
content: Crypto oversight is split mainly between two agencies: the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).  In broad terms, the SEC handles crypto assets that behave like investments in a company. Meanwhile, the CFTC handles assets that behave like commodities, along with futures and other derivatives tied to them. Which […]
summary: The regulation of crypto assets is split between the SEC and CFTC, with the SEC handling assets that behave like investments and the CFTC handling assets that behave like commodities. The distinction is important for understanding which agency has oversight over different types of crypto assets. This regulatory framework is crucial for the crypto industry.

title: JPMorgan says bitcoin outlook sees ‘encouraging sign’ as Strategy boosts cash reserves
content: Strategy's larger cash reserves and improving institutional demand in bitcoin futures are "encouraging signs" for bitcoin, JPMorgan said.
summary: JPMorgan views increasing cash reserves and institutional demand for bitcoin futures as positive signs for the cryptocurrency. This outlook suggests a potential boost for bitcoin. The improvement in investor sentiment may lead to increased adoption and investment.

title: ‘We’re going to get it done’: Crypto bill faces what could be final test over Trump conflicts of interest
content: Optimism is brewing to get a crypto bill passed, with some variations on timing as onlookers wait to see how ethics will shake out.
summary: A crypto bill is approaching a potential final test, with optimism growing that it will be passed, despite concerns over ethics and timing. The bill's outcome is uncertain, but onlookers are watching closely. Ethics and conflicts of interest may impact the bill's passage.

title: SBI Holdings Takes Majority Stake in Singapore’s Coinhako After MAS Approval
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

SBI Holdings Takes Majority Stake in Singapore’s Coinhako After MAS Approval
SBI Holdings has acquired a majority stake in Singapore's Coinhako after MAS approval, strengthening its push to build a cross-border digital asset network across Japan and Southeast Asia.
This post SBI Holdings Takes Majority Stake in Singapore’s Coinhako After MAS Approval first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: SBI Holdings has acquired a majority stake in Singapore's Coinhako after receiving approval from the MAS, expanding its digital asset network in Japan and Southeast Asia. This move strengthens SBI's presence in the region and marks a significant step in its cross-border digital asset strategy. The acquisition is expected to enhance Coinhako's services and offerings.

title: Bitcoin Mining Giant Foundry Asks Miners To Vote on BIP-110 Soft Fork
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Mining Giant Foundry Asks Miners To Vote on BIP-110 Soft Fork
Foundry Digital said it will allow clients who use its Bitcoin mining pool to vote on BIP-110. 
This post Bitcoin Mining Giant Foundry Asks Miners To Vote on BIP-110 Soft Fork first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo and Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Foundry Digital, a Bitcoin mining giant, is allowing its clients to vote on the BIP-110 soft fork through its Bitcoin mining pool. This move enables miners to participate in the decision-making process for the proposed update. The vote will help determine the fate of the BIP-110 soft fork.

title: Bitcoin Price Falls Under $63,000 on U.S.-Iran Strikes and Trump’s China Charge, but Onchain Data Points to Buyers
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Price Falls Under $63,000 on U.S.-Iran Strikes and Trump’s China Charge, but Onchain Data Points to Buyers
Bitcoin price fell below $63,000 as U.S. strikes on Iran and renewed U.S.-China tensions sparked a broader risk-off sell-off, though analysts say resilient onchain data and renewed ETF inflows suggest buyers are quickly stepping back into the market.
This post Bitcoin Price Falls Under $63,000 on U.S.-Iran Strikes and Trump’s China Charge, but Onchain Data Points to Buyers first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitcoin price fell below $63,000 due to US-Iran strikes and US-China tensions, but onchain data indicates buyers are returning to the market. This drop sparked a broader risk-off sell-off. Despite this, analysts remain hopeful due to resilient onchain data and renewed ETF inflows.

title: Crypto.com Secures $400M Investment From Citadel Securities at $20B Valuation
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Crypto.com Secures $400M Investment From Citadel Securities at $20B Valuation
Crypto.com receives $400 million investment from Citadel Securities 
This post Crypto.com Secures $400M Investment From Citadel Securities at $20B Valuation first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.
summary: Crypto.com has secured a $400 million investment from Citadel Securities, valuing the company at $20 billion. This investment highlights the growing interest in cryptocurrency and blockchain technology. The deal underscores the significant role that Crypto.com plays in the crypto space.

title: T. Rowe Price Debuts New ETF With Bitcoin and Crypto Exposure
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

T. Rowe Price Debuts New ETF With Bitcoin and Crypto Exposure
Asset manager T. Rowe Price has debuted a crypto fund giving investors exposure to Bitcoin and other digital assets. 
This post T. Rowe Price Debuts New ETF With Bitcoin and Crypto Exposure first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.
summary: T. Rowe Price has launched a new ETF that provides investors with exposure to Bitcoin and other digital assets, marking a significant development in the crypto space. This fund is expected to give investors a new way to engage with the crypto market. The launch is a notable move by a major asset manager into the crypto sector.

title: Fed Chair Warsh: No Bailout for Crypto Industry in Crisis
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Fed Chair Warsh: No Bailout for Crypto Industry in Crisis
Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said the Fed will not bail out failing crypto firms, insisting the industry must bear its own risks while regulators finalize rules under the GENIUS Act.
This post Fed Chair Warsh: No Bailout for Crypto Industry in Crisis first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh stated that the Fed will not provide a bailout for the crypto industry in times of crisis, emphasizing that the industry must bear its own risks. The decision comes as regulators work to finalize rules under the GENIUS Act. This stance may have significant implications for the crypto industry's future.

title: Bitcoin VC Veterans Launch $40 Million Holding Company Targeting Small Business Acquisitions
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin VC Veterans Launch $40 Million Holding Company Targeting Small Business Acquisitions
Lyn Alden is helping debut a permanent capital company to buys profitable small businesses and put them on a Bitcoin standard.
This post Bitcoin VC Veterans Launch $40 Million Holding Company Targeting Small Business Acquisitions first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.
summary: Bitcoin VC veterans have launched a $40 million holding company to acquire small businesses and transition them to a Bitcoin standard, with Lyn Alden involved in the initiative. The company aims to buy profitable small businesses, marking a new strategy in the crypto space. This move could have implications for the adoption of Bitcoin in mainstream commerce.

title: Breez Partners With Turnkey to Bring Non-Custodial Bitcoin to Backend-Run Apps
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Breez Partners With Turnkey to Bring Non-Custodial Bitcoin to Backend-Run Apps
Breez has partnered with Turnkey to let backend-run apps integrate non-custodial bitcoin wallets at scale, allowing users to retain control of their funds without requiring companies to hold private keys or redesign their existing infrastructure.
This post Breez Partners With Turnkey to Bring Non-Custodial Bitcoin to Backend-Run Apps first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Breez has partnered with Turnkey to enable backend-run apps to integrate non-custodial bitcoin wallets, allowing users to control their funds without companies holding private keys. This partnership allows for scalability and doesn't require infrastructure redesign. Users can now have more control over their bitcoin wallets.

title: The Download: OpenAI unveils GPT-Red and heat pumps rise in the US
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. This innovation aims to enhance the security and reliability of OpenAI's technologies. GPT-Red is used to simulate hacking attempts and identify potential vulnerabilities.

title: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
content: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red made the model its most robust release yet. GPT-Red automates…
summary: OpenAI has developed GPT-Red, an LLM super-hacker, to test and improve the security of its other models against cyberattacks, resulting in the robust GPT-5.6 release. GPT-Red acts as a sparring partner to boost the defenses of OpenAI's models. This breakthrough aims to enhance the safety of OpenAI's models.

title: The Download: a useful quantum machine and a record-breaking subsea tunnel
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks like a data…

This innovation is part of the daily technology newsletter, The Download. The quantum machine has the potential to significantly impact various fields, including possibly AI and crypto.

title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings, and the future of world models
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show —James O’Donnell When Anthropic announced last week that it had found a new window into its models’ “internal thoughts” as they reason through answers,…

The discovery is part of ongoing research into the development of more advanced AI models. This breakthrough has the potential to improve our understanding of AI decision-making processes.

title: PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light
content: The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks like a data center crossed with an ice cream factory. Inside will be some 100 stainless-steel cabinets, each about six feet tall and connected to a supply of liquid helium that keeps them only a few degrees above absolute zero.…
summary: PsiQuantum is building a massive quantum computer using light, which will be housed in a large room with 100 stainless-steel cabinets kept near absolute zero using liquid helium. This machine has the potential to change the world. The project is still in development, but its implications are significant.

title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
content: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and heady research. It’s looking into whether AI models can feel pain, for example,…

The company's latest discovery has garnered attention, but its implications are still being debated. Anthropic's research pushes the boundaries of AI capabilities and understanding.

title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
content: "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside."
summary: Director Christopher Nolan has publicly voiced his concern regarding artificial intelligence, labeling it an 'obvious Trojan horse'. He suggested that the inherent risks or hidden intentions of AI are apparent, stating that 'everybody knows the Greeks are inside'.

title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
content: Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more.
summary: Current AI, a non-profit organization, is actively working to create a 'World Wide Web of AI' that is free and accessible to everyone, ensuring no culture is left behind. They have reported significant progress in areas such as AI chat and device compatibility.

title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
content: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."
summary: Chinese company Moonshot AI has released a new version of its Kimi model, sparking concerns about the potential implications of advanced AI. The concept of 'full AI communism' has been raised, implying a significant shift in the way AI systems are developed and utilized. This development has notable implications for the AI community.

title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
content: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.
summary: Databricks has transformed into an AI company and published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding, achieving a valuation of $188B. This development solidifies its position in the AI industry. The company's focus on AI has led to significant growth and recognition.

title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
content: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.
summary: India's smartphone market is experiencing a slowdown due to the AI boom, which is changing consumer electronics in terms of pricing, demand, and corporate strategy. The AI-driven shift is impacting the market, with significant effects on consumer behavior and company approaches. This change highlights the growing influence of AI on various industries.

title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
content: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]
summary: Apple has filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging misconduct and claiming over 400 former Apple employees now work at OpenAI, which could disrupt OpenAI's potential IPO plans. The lawsuit's timing is particularly noteworthy given OpenAI's reported interest in going public. The outcome of the lawsuit could have significant implications for OpenAI's future

title: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
content: Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from relying on websites using robots.txt alone to actively block unauthorized AI training.
summary: Patreon is working with Cloudflare to block AI bots that scrape creators' content without permission, shifting away from relying on robots.txt alone. This move aims to strengthen defenses against AI scraping. The change is intended to protect creators' content from unauthorized AI training.

title: Apple’s lawsuit couldn’t come at a worse time for OpenAI
content: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]
summary: Apple has filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging misconduct and claiming that over 400 former Apple employees now work at the company, which comes at a bad time as OpenAI is reportedly considering an IPO. OpenAI's response has been cautious. The lawsuit's outcome may impact OpenAI's future plans.

title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
content: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.
summary: A $400 million chip-backed loan indicates a shift towards inference chips for AI infrastructure, marking the next wave of deals. This investment suggests growing interest in specialized AI hardware. The loan is backed by GPU financiers turning to inference chips.

title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
content: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.
summary: Google Vids now allows users to create personalized AI videos starring a digital version of themselves, utilizing AI avatars and Gemini Omni-powered tools for video generation and editing. This feature enables users to produce custom videos from prompts and reference images. The integration of AI avatars enhances the video creation experience.

title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
content: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.
summary: Roblox has launched a new AI-powered game creation feature called Build, allowing users to generate basic games using a single text prompt in its mobile app. This feature simplifies the game development process, making it more accessible to users. The AI technology enables fast and easy creation of games with minimal effort.

title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
content: With this new update, Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions and into completing tasks across the apps they use regularly.
summary: Google has updated its AI Mode to enable interaction with select apps, allowing users to complete tasks beyond just answering questions. This expansion aims to integrate AI functionality into everyday app usage. The update enhances the capabilities of Google's AI Mode.

title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
content: Drawing on more than a decade spent helping build some of the world's most influential AI systems, including research that later informed the development of ChatGPT, Andrew Dai explains why he believes visual AI is one of the next major frontiers in artificial intelligence.
summary: A former DeepMind researcher, Andrew Dai, has raised $300M at a pre-seed valuation for his new venture, focused on visual AI, which he believes is a major frontier in artificial intelligence. His experience includes helping build influential AI systems that informed the development of ChatGPT. The venture has yet to launch a product.

title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
content:
TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted by social media consultant Matt Navarra. The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells The Verge. YouTube has been working on a similar tool and recently made it available to all adult users.
Creators who are part of TikTok's test and want to use the tool will first have to verify their identity with a company called Jumio. You'll have to do a real-time selfie scan and an ID check, but Kizer says that "TikTok does not retain ID documents, and facial informati …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: TikTok is testing a tool that detects AI-generated likenesses, allowing creators to report them, with the goal of combating AI-generated content. The tool is currently being tested with some US creators and requires identity verification. This move follows YouTube's similar initiative to tackle AI-generated content.

title: Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
content:
Apple is suing OpenAI. The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done. So what does Apple really want here, and why is it picking such a public fight with OpenAI?
On this episode of The Vergecast, Nilay and David go through the lawsuit, and look at Apple's history of splashy litigation to determine whether Apple is worried about a possible competitor or simply looking to capitalize on a weak moment for OpenAI. All this is happening as Apple ships the public betas of its new software, headlined by the new Siri AI, and we have though …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, with many experts thinking the allegations are standard practices, and the lawsuit's true intentions are unclear, possibly due to Apple seeing OpenAI as a competitor or capitalizing on a weak moment. The lawsuit is happening as Apple ships its new software featuring a new Siri AI. The Verge's Nilay and David analyzed the lawsuit to understand Apple's motivations.

title: New York governor says she’s using AI to analyze ‘every single rule’ in the state
content:
New York Governor Kathy Hochul might have just signed a moratorium on new AI data centers in the state, but she's not against using the technology herself. During an interview with Bloomberg's Odd Lots podcast, Hochul said that her team is using "AI to analyze every single rule, regulation, [and] policy" to check for outdated legislation.
Some of the antiquated laws mentioned by Hochul in the interview include a $25 fee required to take a dog hunting, or a stipulation that pregnant people need a permit to work after midnight. Hochul added that it "probably would have taken five years at the staff level" to review all of the laws in the stat …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: New York Governor Kathy Hochul is utilizing AI to review and analyze the state's rules and regulations to identify outdated legislation, including a $25 fee for taking a dog hunting and a permit requirement for pregnant people working after midnight. This effort aims to streamline and modernize the state's laws. The use of AI is expected to significantly reduce the time required to review the laws.

title: Claude can now use your 1Password credentials for you
content:
1Password has launched a new browser integration for Claude that allows the Anthropic chatbot to access stored security credentials like usernames and passwords. The 1Password for Claude feature means that users can authorize Claude to complete multi-step tasks like booking travel and managing online accounts on their behalf without having to manually input their login credentials, but without actually exposing security information to Anthropic's AI models, according to 1Password.
That's made possible by a new "zero-exposure security framework" developed by 1Password, which works by injecting the required credentials for each task through a …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: 1Password has launched a browser integration for Claude, an Anthropic chatbot, allowing it to access stored security credentials to complete tasks without exposing sensitive information. This is made possible by 1Password's new zero-exposure security framework. Claude can now perform tasks like booking travel and managing online accounts on behalf of users.

title: Google ordered to open Android and Search to rivals in Europe
content:
Google must give rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to key parts of Android and Google Search after the European Union ordered the company to comply with the bloc's digital antitrust rules. 
The two decisions, handed down Thursday, could weaken Google's control over two of the tech industry's most important platforms and have far-reaching consequences for the company, shape the future of its AI tool Gemini, and open up new opportunities for rivals to gain ground. Google has until January 2027 to begin sharing search data and July 2027 to implement changes to Android.
The rulings stem from technical regulatory proceedings …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The European Union has ordered Google to give rival AI assistants and search engines greater access to Android and Google Search, potentially weakening Google's control over these platforms. This move could have far-reaching consequences for the company and create new opportunities for rivals. Google must comply with the EU's digital antitrust rules by January and July 2027.

title: Computer cops
content:
I stood before a hulking glass and brick structure in the heart of Fort Worth, Texas. Thousands gathered inside to see what had been billed as "the future of policing in the digital age." As press, I was prohibited from entering, but from a number of nearby locations, I met with attendees who told me what was being sold within. And I learned that AI is threatening to seize the very heart of policing in America.
The promise of AI at this year's International Association of Chiefs of Police (IACP) Technology Conference focused on automating routine parts of the job, which also happen to be critical steps in the legal process. It's a similar …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The International Association of Chiefs of Police Technology Conference showcased AI's potential to automate routine policing tasks, which are critical to the legal process. Attendees learned about AI's role in the future of policing in America. The conference highlighted AI's impact on the digital age of policing.

title: Will AI fix prior authorization—or make it worse?
content: The government is piloting a program that uses AI for insurance-coverage decisions.
summary: The government is piloting a program that leverages AI for insurance-coverage decisions, particularly concerning prior authorization. This initiative raises questions about whether AI will improve the efficiency of these processes or introduce new challenges.

title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
content: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.
summary: The EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android, which Google claims could compromise user privacy and security. This move is part of the EU's efforts to regulate big tech companies. The changes aim to promote competition and transparency in the tech industry.

title: xAI can’t deny Grok makes CSAM anymore. So it’s suing users.
content: Elon Musk's xAI files first lawsuit against Grok user accused of making child sex images.
summary: Elon Musk's xAI is suing a user of its Grok platform for allegedly creating child sex images, marking the company's first lawsuit. This comes after xAI claimed it could no longer deny that Grok was being used for this purpose. The lawsuit highlights the challenges faced by AI companies in regulating user-generated content.

title: Fear of humanoid robots spurs human workers to strike at Hyundai auto factory
content: Hyundai aims to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots starting with US factories in 2028.
summary: Hyundai plans to deploy 25,000 Atlas robots in its factories, starting with US locations in 2028, prompting human workers to strike due to fears of job displacement by humanoid robots. The strike highlights concerns about the impact of automation on employment. The introduction of Atlas robots is expected to significantly change the factory workforce.

title: Linus Torvalds to critics of AI coding in Linux: "Fork it. Or just walk away."
content: Creator says he will "very loudly ignore" those arguing for a ban on AI tools.
summary: Linus Torvalds, the creator of Linux, has responded to critics of AI coding in Linux, stating that he will ignore those arguing for a ban on AI tools and telling them to fork the project or walk away. This response indicates a strong stance in favor of AI integration in Linux. The decision may have significant implications for the future of Linux development.

title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
content: Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.
summary: A lawsuit claims that Meta used AI to make layoff decisions, potentially targeting workers with disabilities and medical problems. Meta denies the allegations, stating that humans made the termination decisions. The lawsuit raises concerns about the use of AI in employment decisions.

title: New York bans data center construction for a year, rattling AI industry
content: New York’s data center moratorium may become the blueprint for anti-AI movement.
summary: New York has implemented a one-year ban on data center construction, which may have significant implications for the AI industry and could potentially serve as a model for an anti-AI movement. This moratorium may hinder the growth and development of AI technologies that rely on data centers. The ban's impact on the AI industry is likely to be closely watched by experts and stakeholders.

title: Apple sues OpenAI after ex-engineer allegedly used bug to steal trade secrets
content: OpenAI accused of conspiring with former Apple employees to steal trade secrets.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, alleging that a former Apple engineer used a bug to steal trade secrets and conspired with OpenAI. The engineer allegedly used the stolen information to benefit OpenAI. The lawsuit claims a breach of contract and misappropriation of trade secrets.

title: Now, defenders are embracing the prompt injection, too
content: "Context bombing" tricks hacking agents into shutting down before they can do harm.
summary: Defenders are using a technique called 'context bombing' or 'prompt injection' to trick hacking agents into shutting down before they can cause harm. This method involves providing misleading context to the hacking agents. The technique is showing promise in preventing cyber attacks.

title: Simulating everything, sort of: The promise and limits of world models
content: Experts explain how they work, what they can do, and what's still unsettled.
summary: Experts discuss the capabilities and limitations of world models, which are simulating complex systems. These models can mimic various aspects of reality, but their potential and current state are still being explored. World models have significant implications for fields like artificial intelligence.

title: OpenAI wants its new tool to do your work for you and with you
content: Rebranded Codex promises independent workflows that can run "for hours if needed."
summary: OpenAI's new tool, rebranded Codex, aims to assist and automate workflows, allowing for independent operation for extended periods. This advancement has the potential to significantly impact productivity. Codex can run for hours if needed, making it a valuable asset for various tasks.

title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
content: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.
summary: A preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of using humanoid robots controlled by surgeons to perform operations, with a world-first operation being conducted on live pigs. This trial aims to assess the potential of humanoid robots in surgery. The use of robots in surgery could potentially revolutionize the field.

title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
content: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.
summary: OpenAI is facing potential sanctions for allegedly hiding and deleting ChatGPT logs in a copyright dispute with the New York Times. The case could have significant implications for AI companies and their handling of user data. OpenAI's actions may be seen as a misstep in its legal battle with news organizations.

title: Kraken says simpler options can unlock crypto's next derivatives market
content: The Wyoming-based firm launched USD-settled bitcoin and ether options, saying product design, not demand, has held back adoption.
summary: Kraken, a Wyoming-based firm, has launched USD-settled bitcoin and ether options, asserting that product design, not demand, has previously stifled market adoption. The company believes simpler options can unlock the crypto derivatives market's next growth phase.

title: Crypto executives say digital native generations may never need a bank account
content: Steakhouse Financial co-founder Adrian Cachinero says digital-native generations may rely less on banks, while Binance says younger users are already driving crypto adoption in emerging markets.
summary: Crypto executives believe that digital-native generations may never need traditional bank accounts, instead relying on digital financial systems. Adrian Cachinero of Steakhouse Financial and Binance both note that younger users are already leading the charge in crypto adoption, particularly within emerging markets.

title: Trump targets Brazil's payments system while dollar stablecoins are quietly overtaking country's payments
content: Washington views Brazil’s promotion of non-dollar payment channels, including Pix and the growth of stablecoins, as a potential threat to dollar-based trade, even as dollar-linked stablecoins account for about 90% of Brazil's crypto transactions.
summary: Washington views Brazil's promotion of non-dollar payment channels, such as Pix and stablecoins, as a potential threat to dollar-based trade. This concern arises despite dollar-linked stablecoins already constituting about 90% of Brazil's crypto transactions, significantly impacting the country's payment landscape.

title: Here is why a massive $1.6 billion in crypto liquidity is sitting idle and wasting away
content: About $542 million weekly sat outside active trading ranges, meaning this capital earned zero fees and provided no market depth.
summary: Around $1.6 billion in cryptocurrency liquidity is currently sitting idle, with approximately $542 million per week positioned outside active trading ranges. This substantial capital is not earning any fees and fails to contribute to market depth, indicating a significant inefficiency in the crypto market.

title: Massive bitcoin call spreads target $72,000 by month end, right when the Fed meets
content: Large traders are betting on a BTC price rise to $72,000 by the end of the month, latest options market flow suggests.
summary: Large traders are betting on Bitcoin (BTC) reaching a price of $72,000 by the end of the month, according to significant call spreads observed in the options market. This bullish market sentiment coincides with an upcoming Federal Reserve meeting.

title: Cardano hands core development to outside teams in decentralization push
content: Input Output will transfer control of the Haskell node, Plutus, Hydra and other components as founder Charles Hoskinson says the network must change and start growing again.
summary: Cardano's core developer, Input Output, is transferring control of key components like the Haskell node, Plutus, and Hydra to outside teams. This move is part of a broader decentralization push, as founder Charles Hoskinson emphasizes the need for the network to evolve and resume growth.

title: Risk-off wave drags bitcoin below $63,000 as AI selloff spreads from stocks to crypto
content: Crypto fell as AI stock fatigue and U.S.-Iran tensions weighed on sentiment, though a technical measure hints at oversold conditions heading into the weekend.
summary: Bitcoin fell below $63,000, dragged down by a general 'risk-off' sentiment. This downturn was attributed to fatigue in AI stocks and rising U.S.-Iran tensions impacting market sentiment. However, a technical indicator suggests oversold conditions for crypto heading into the weekend.

title: Upbit parent Dunamu faces South Korean regulator sanctions process: Report
content: Korea’s Virtual Asset User Protection Act lacks explicit sanctions provisions for hacking and similar incidents, clouding the scope of penalties facing the Upbit operator Dunamu.
summary: South Korean regulators have reportedly begun a sanctions process against Dunamu, a major virtual asset operator. This action highlights a significant gap in the country's Virtual Asset User Protection Act, which lacks explicit provisions for penalties related to hacking and computer system incidents, leading to uncertainty regarding potential sanctions.

title: Kaspersky identifies malware framework targeting crypto investors
content: Cybersecurity company Kaspersky said a newly identified malware framework is targeting cryptocurrency investors through social engineering tactics and trojanized GitHub apps.
summary: Cybersecurity firm Kaspersky has identified a new malware framework specifically targeting cryptocurrency investors. This framework employs social engineering tactics and trojanized GitHub applications to compromise victims and steal their assets.

title: Bitcoin bottom countdown nears 50 days after BTC supply in loss passed 50%
content: Bitcoin supply in loss hit 50% nearly 50 days ago, mimicking the countdown to BTC price bear-market bottoms.
summary: For nearly 50 days, over half of the Bitcoin supply has been held at a loss, a metric historically associated with previous bear market bottoms. This trend suggests a potential countdown to a market turnaround for BTC as it mimics past bear market cycles.

title: Ordinals advocate Leonidas proposes new Bitcoin client: ‘$DOG Mode’
content: “Over time the economic incentives will drive $DOG Mode’s adoption and force Bitcoin Core to stop gatekeeping and allow these completely valid transactions,” said Leonidas.
summary: Ordinals advocate Leonidas has proposed a new Bitcoin client named '$DOG Mode'. Leonidas believes this client will gain adoption through economic incentives, ultimately compelling Bitcoin Core to permit what he considers completely valid transactions.

title: Crypto exchange Bybit launches in Indonesia after NOBI acquisition
content: The launch gives Bybit a locally operated platform in one of Asia’s largest crypto markets, home to more than 21 million crypto exchange users.
summary: Crypto exchange Bybit has officially launched its operations in Indonesia following its acquisition of the local platform NOBI. This strategic move grants Bybit a locally operated presence in one of Asia's largest crypto markets, which serves over 21 million crypto exchange users.

title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
content: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.
summary: Venture capitalist Neil Rimer, co-founder of Index Ventures, predicts that the significant wealth currently being generated by the artificial intelligence sector in Silicon Valley will eventually need to be redistributed. He suggests this redistribution of AI-derived wealth could occur either voluntarily or involuntarily.

title: I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
content:
1010Benja won’t apologize for using AI. | Image: 1010Benja / Instagram	

I would never go so far as to say there's no place for AI in music (I'm a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be offensively boring, especially the outputs of Suno. So I'm having a bit of a tough time processing the fact that I actually quite enjoy 1010Benja's "Semiramis' Dream."
Benja has been unapologetic about his use of generative AI on his latest EP, Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose… The other three tracks can't quite hold a candle to what you find on 2024's Ten Total. But the opener "Semiramis' Dream" is infectious. It explodes out of the speakers with a jungle beat and Be …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The author, usually critical of generative AI music, expresses surprise and enjoyment for 1010Benja's song "Semiramis' Dream," which was created using AI, specifically Suno. 1010Benja has been open about using generative AI in his latest EP, and this particular track challenges the author's general negative perception of AI-produced music.

title: Dave Eggers told OpenAI staff  that ChatGPT was ‘silencing an entire generation’
content:
Dave Eggers attends the "The Turning Point: To Be Destroyed" premiere. | Image: John Lamparski/Getty Images for Tribeca Festival	

Last year, Sam Altman invited author Dave Eggers to give a talk to around 200 OpenAI staffers. The man has written countless novels, screenplays, pieces of journalism, started McSweeney's, and founded multiple schools and nonprofits that support writers and the arts more broadly. So one might expect he'd roll into the company's offices and offer tips on being relentlessly prolific, or how to excel in multiple fields. Instead, he apparently laced into the company. According to the Financial Times, Eggers told the staff:
"The effect of ChatGPT on educators' lives is catastrophic. Whether you intended to do it or not, you've made every teache …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Author Dave Eggers, invited by Sam Altman, criticized ChatGPT during a talk to OpenAI staff, stating its impact on educators is "catastrophic." He reportedly argued that the AI tool is "silencing an entire generation," challenging the company despite being expected to offer advice on productivity.

title: Live markets: Bitcoin returns to $63,000 as Nasdaq trims large early loss
content: A deepening global selloff in chipmakers dragged risk assets lower, pulling bitcoin back from the $65,000 level it reached on this week's soft inflation print.
summary: Bitcoin has slipped below $63,000, dragged down by a deepening global selloff in chipmakers that impacted broader risk assets. This decline follows its brief rally to $65,000 earlier in the week after a favorable inflation report.

title: Ether falls twice as hard as bitcoin and HYPE drops 10% as the chip trade unwinds
content: Japan's Nikkei had its worst day since March. Ether is still the only major holding a gain on the week, barely.
summary: Ether experienced a significant drop, falling twice as hard as Bitcoin, amidst a broader market downturn that also saw 'HYPE' token decline by 10% and Japan's Nikkei have its worst day since March. Despite these losses, Ether was still barely holding onto a weekly gain among major assets.

title: Galaxy lands 15-year Texas Tech stadium naming rights deal
content: Galaxy Digital will rename Texas Tech’s football stadium under a 15-year agreement, expanding its West Texas presence as the state attracts growing crypto investment.
summary: Galaxy Digital has secured a 15-year naming rights deal for Texas Tech's football stadium, increasing its presence in West Texas as the state sees growing crypto investment. This deal marks a significant expansion of the company's influence in the region. The agreement is part of the state's growing appeal to crypto investors.

title: OKX Europe lets users convert USDT to MiCA-compliant USDC
content: The feature offers European customers a voluntary path away from Tether’s USDT as MiCA rules reshape the region’s stablecoin market.
summary: OKX Europe now allows users to convert USDT to USDC, providing a voluntary alternative for European customers as MiCA regulations impact the stablecoin market. This move is in response to the changing regulatory landscape in Europe. The conversion aims to offer a compliant option for users.

title: Senator Warren requests 2026 reporting for Trump’s crypto earnings after $1.4B disclosure
content: With the Senate likely voting on a crypto bill within days, Elizabeth Warren asked for information on Donald Trump’s earnings between January and July ahead of a 2027 deadline.
summary: Senator Elizabeth Warren has requested information on Donald Trump's crypto earnings from January to July 2026, ahead of a 2027 deadline. This comes as the Senate is set to vote on a crypto bill in the coming days. The request follows a recent disclosure of Trump's $1.4B earnings.

title: Bitcoin price sags under $62.5K as Iran strikes add to US stocks pressure
content: Bitcoin saw a key rejection at local highs before reversing lower, moving with stocks for a second day as US-Iran war downside took its toll.
summary: Bitcoin experienced a significant rejection at its recent peak, subsequently declining and mirroring stock market movements for the second consecutive day. This downturn is attributed to the negative impact of the US-Iran conflict, pushing its price below $62.5K.

title: HSBC wins Bank of England approval to enter Digital Securities Sandbox
content: The Bank of England approved HSBC Orion to go live in its Digital Securities Sandbox, with the first Digital Gilt Instrument transaction expected in the first quarter of 2027.
summary: HSBC has received approval from the Bank of England to enter its Digital Securities Sandbox, with plans to conduct its first Digital Gilt Instrument transaction in early 2027. This marks a significant step in the development of digital securities. HSBC Orion will be testing its digital securities capabilities in the sandbox.

title: The British Virgin Islands are a top crypto hub no one ever talks about: Here’s why
content: Kraken, Bitstamp, 1inch and Bitfinex have all set up shop in the British Virgin Islands — but you might find it tricky to book an on-site meeting with one of their executives.
summary: The British Virgin Islands have become a popular hub for cryptocurrency companies, with major players like Kraken and Bitstamp setting up operations there, despite being a lesser-known destination. The islands' favorable business environment and regulations may be contributing to their appeal. This development could have significant implications for the global crypto industry.

title: US indicts crypto investor over alleged $20M fraud scheme
content: Federal prosecutors allege the South Dakota investor used false promises to raise money, repaid earlier investors with new funds and laundered proceeds through crypto exchanges.
summary: US federal prosecutors have indicted a South Dakota investor for an alleged $20 million fraud scheme. The investor is accused of making false promises to raise funds, repaying earlier investors with new money (a Ponzi scheme), and laundering the proceeds through cryptocurrency exchanges.

title: Trump Media sells Wall Street low-latency access to Trump posts
content: The paid Truth API is aimed at high-frequency trading firms seeking “the fastest” access to market-moving Truth Social posts, including those from US President Donald Trump.
summary: Trump Media is selling low-latency access to Trump's social media posts through the paid Truth API, targeting high-frequency trading firms. This service aims to provide the fastest access to market-moving posts, including those from Donald Trump. The move could be significant for traders seeking to capitalize on market trends.

title: Balaji seeks Malaysia deal, threatens exit after Network School probe
content: “If we are not welcome, there are many other countries that would welcome us,” said Network School founder Balaji Srinivasan in a message to the Malaysian prime minister.
summary: Balaji Srinivasan, founder of Network School, is threatening to exit Malaysia if his venture is not welcomed following a government probe. He has communicated a desire for a deal with the Malaysian prime minister, suggesting other countries would be receptive to his organization.

title: Bitcoin Japan, which holds no bitcoin, taps EVO Fund in planned $60 million raise to finally buy some
content: A prior December raise instead funded stakes tied to SpaceX and Figure AI, which the company says are its two AI infrastructure investments to date.
summary: Bitcoin Japan is planning to raise $60 million to buy bitcoin, after a previous raise was used to fund stakes in SpaceX and Figure AI. The company currently holds no bitcoin. This move is part of the company's efforts to finally acquire the cryptocurrency.

title: CryptoQuant says Strategy still needs disciplined bitcoin buying and selling framework
content: CryptoQuant said Michael Saylor's Strategy still needs still needs clearer rules for when to buy and sell bitcoin.
summary: CryptoQuant criticized Michael Saylor's bitcoin investment strategy, stating it lacks a clear framework for buying and selling. The strategy needs more discipline and defined rules. This critique may impact bitcoin investment approaches.

title: Bitcoin slides toward $63,000 as Coinbase premium stays negative for a record 60 days
content: Bitcoin fell near $63,000 as chip stocks sold off, the Coinbase premium stayed negative a record 60 days, and ETF flows remain thin.
summary: Bitcoin's price has fallen to near $63,000, with the Coinbase premium remaining negative for a record 60 days and ETF flows staying thin. The decline is also attributed to a sell-off in chip stocks. This downward trend may be of concern to investors in the cryptocurrency market.

title: Galaxy Digital puts its name on Texas Tech football stadium in 15-year partnership
content: The deal's financial terms were not disclosed, but Yahoo Sports reported the agreement is worth more than $70 million.
summary: Galaxy Digital has partnered with Texas Tech in a 15-year deal to put its name on the university's football stadium, with the agreement valued at over $70 million. The financial terms were not publicly disclosed. This deal marks a significant investment by Galaxy Digital in sports marketing.

title: SBI Holdings completes majority acquisition of Singapore crypto platform Coinhako following MAS approval
content: SBI Holdings acquired Singapore's Coinhako after MAS approval, folding the licensed exchange into its expanding digital asset network.
summary: SBI Holdings has completed its majority acquisition of Coinhako, a Singapore-based crypto platform, after receiving approval from the Monetary Authority of Singapore. This move expands SBI Holdings' digital asset network. The acquisition brings a licensed exchange under SBI Holdings' control.

title: Bitcoin Sentiment Is Turning Bullish — But It’s Too Early to Celebrate: Report
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Sentiment Is Turning Bullish — But It’s Too Early to Celebrate: Report
CoinShares has said that while investors have put money in crypto funds again, the Bitcoin price may still struggle.
This post Bitcoin Sentiment Is Turning Bullish — But It’s Too Early to Celebrate: Report first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.
summary: Bitcoin sentiment is turning bullish as investors put money back into crypto funds, but the Bitcoin price may still struggle. According to CoinShares, it's too early to celebrate. The report indicates a potential shift in investor attitude towards Bitcoin.

title: Ocean Mining VP Jason Hughes: BIP-110 on Track to Fail as Miner Signaling Stays Below 1%
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Ocean Mining VP Jason Hughes: BIP-110 on Track to Fail as Miner Signaling Stays Below 1%
Ocean Mining’s VP of Development and Engineering Jason Hughes argues BIP-110 lacks measurable consensus, with node signaling at 7-15% and hashrate support under 1% ahead of the critical block 961632 window.
This post Ocean Mining VP Jason Hughes: BIP-110 on Track to Fail as Miner Signaling Stays Below 1% first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Jason Hughes.
summary: Jason Hughes, Ocean Mining's VP, believes BIP-110 will fail due to lack of consensus, with node signaling at 7-15% and hashrate support under 1%. The proposal is nearing a critical block window. Hughes argues that the current support is not enough for the proposal to pass.

title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
content: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?

The article touches on the idea that with so much data being recorded, it's unclear who is actually reading and utilizing it. This hack highlights the need for transparency in data collection and usage.

content: DoorDash is opening a limited beta of dd-cli, a command-line tool that lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal, marking another step toward software designed for AI agents instead of just humans.

title: Google is renaming NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
content:
Google is giving its AI note-taking app a new name. The company announced on Thursday that NotebookLM is becoming Gemini Notebook, but will remain a standalone app even as it integrates more deeply across Gemini and Google Search.
Google first revealed Gemini Notebook - then called Project Tailwind - in May 2023 before widely releasing the app just months later. Over the past few years, Google has been adding new features to the app to help organize and make sense of your notes, such as the ability to summarize them as AI podcasts, narrated slideshows, and TikTok-style clips. It recently started letting users connect their notebooks to the  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Google is renaming its AI note-taking app from NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook, with the app to remain standalone despite deeper integration with Gemini and Google Search. The app has been updated with features to organize notes, including summarizing them as AI podcasts and clips. The name change follows the app's wide release after its initial reveal in May 2023.

content: The FireSat program can spot wildfires that other satellites miss.

title: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising

While these forecasts are something that most people glance at for two seconds, weather predictions influence major strategic decisions in many industries, with real money, livelihoods, and even actual lives at stake. Farmers use…
summary: Weather forecasts significantly impact various industries such as aviation, energy, and agriculture, influencing strategic decisions with substantial financial and human implications. The accuracy of these forecasts is crucial, and any disruption could have far-reaching consequences. However, the article highlights the rising risk of weather data sabotage.

title: There’s a lot of hype around perimenopause. Don’t buy it.

Perhaps it’s my age, but these days, both my algorithm and my conversations with friends increasingly swing toward perimenopause. Menopause is defined as the life stage that…

Perimenopause is the stage leading up to menopause, and its definition and implications are being explored. The article seems to be skeptical of the hype surrounding perimenopause.
